info.name: DriveBai API — Full Demo
info.description: End-to-end demo of the DriveBai backend. Run the requests in order inside each folder. IDs and tokens are captured automatically via test scripts. Pre-request hook at collection level auto-generates unique demo emails on first run. See demo/README.md for the live-demo narration script.

exec: // Auto-generate unique demo emails once per environment

item.name: 01 · Health & Setup
item.description: Confirm the API is up before starting the demo.

request.description: Confirms the API is up and the database is reachable. Expect 200 'ok'.
Returns the OpenAPI YAML spec. Use for quick reference or import into any OpenAPI tool.
Serves the Swagger UI at /docs — open in a browser to explore the spec interactively.

item.name: 02 · Auth — Classic
item.description: Classic email+password registration, login, and refresh. No email verification step is required — the backend auto-verifies on register.

request.description: Register as a car owner. Server returns JWT pair immediately (no email verification needed). Token is saved to `accessTokenOwner`.
Register as a driver. JWT saved to `accessTokenDriver`.
Log in as existing owner and refresh the stored owner token.
Log in as existing driver.
Rotate the owner's refresh token. Old refresh is revoked; both access and refresh are replaced in the environment.
Return the authenticated owner's profile.
Return the authenticated driver's profile.
Revoke the owner's refresh token. After this, refresh will fail. Run Login again to re-issue.

item.name: 02b · Auth — Passwordless OTP (optional)
item.description: Passwordless login via email OTP. In dev mode the code is printed to the backend console (look for the '📧 LOGIN OTP EMAIL' banner in the server log).

request.description: Send a 6-digit OTP code to the email. In dev mode (no MAILERSEND_API_KEY), the code is printed to the server console — copy it from the backend log into `otpCode` env variable.
Verify the OTP. If the email is new, response returns `{kind:'register', registration_token}`. If existing, returns a JWT pair directly.
Finish registration using the token returned from Verify OTP.

item.name: 03 · Owner — Car Lifecycle
item.description: Flow: Create Car (pending) → Upload cover photo (auto-publishes) → Update / Add more photos & docs / Pause when needed. All auth'd routes require the owner's Bearer token.

request.description: Create a listing. Starts as `status: pending` — invisible in /listings until the first photo is uploaded.

body.raw:
{
  "make": "Toyota",
  "model": "Camry",
  "year": 2022,
  "title": "2022 Toyota Camry \u2014 weekly rental",
  "description": "Clean family sedan, newly serviced.",
  "body_type": "sedan",
  "fuel_type": "gas",
  "mileage": 35000,
  "address": "Almaty, Dostyk ave.",
  "neighborhood": "Samal",
  "latitude": 43.2389,
  "longitude": 76.9595,
  "is_for_rent": true,
  "weekly_rent_price": 280,
  "is_for_sale": false,
  "min_years_licensed": 2,
  "deposit_amount": 500,
  "insurance_coverage": "full_coverage"
}

request.description: Owner's own cars (pending + available + paused).
Owner view of a single car, with photos & documents embedded.

Upload a photo in the `cover_front` slot. When a car is in `pending` status, uploading the cover photo auto-transitions status to `available` and the listing becomes visible in GET /listings. You MUST pick a file before sending.
Returns photos grouped by slot_type.
Additional photo slots: right | left | back | dashboard.
Partial update — send only the fields you want to change.
Dedicated endpoint for location-only updates.
Toggle the paused state. When paused, the listing is hidden from /listings.
Car documents: inspection | registration | permit | insurance. JPEG/PNG/PDF up to 10MB.
List uploaded car documents.
Optional — uploads the user's profile photo. Advances onboarding for role=owner.

item.name: 04 · Driver — Onboarding & Modes
item.description: Documents, onboarding completion, profile photo, and switching between driver/owner modes on one account.

request.description: Upload driver's license. Pick a PDF or image (JPEG/PNG). After both docs are uploaded, onboarding_status advances to `documents_uploaded`.
Upload vehicle registration document (or any stand-in PDF for demo).
List all user documents.
Marks driver onboarding complete. Requires both documents uploaded (409 otherwise).
Set the driver's profile photo.
Show driver/owner profiles for this user. Both roles can exist for one account.

item.name: Create Owner Profile on Driver Account

request.description: Lazily create a second profile. Idempotent.

item.name: Switch to Owner Mode (issues new JWT)

request.description: Switch the active profile. Server returns a fresh JWT with the new role embedded. Switching to `driver` requires docs uploaded.
Update profile fields. Role changes here do NOT flip active mode — use /me/active-profile for that.

item.name: 05 · Listings — Public Browse
item.description: Public browse endpoint a driver sees before logging in.

request.description: No auth required. Returns all listings with status=available.
Full-text search across make / model / title.
Filter by listing status. Other values: available, paused, pending, rented.
Idempotent like. Server stores one row per (user, listing).
Returns {liked_listing_ids: [...]} — driver's favourites.
Idempotent unlike — safe to call even if the like doesn't exist.

item.name: 07 · Lease Requests + Chat (happy path)
item.description: The core booking flow: driver creates a lease request → chat is auto-created → conversation → owner accepts.

request.description: Creates a lease request AND the chat between driver and owner in one call. Server broadcasts `lease_request_created` via WebSocket to the owner. Driver docs (if uploaded) are auto-shared with the owner for this request.
Both participants can list the chat's lease requests.
Driver's shared documents (e.g. license, registration) attached to this lease request. Empty if the driver uploaded no documents before creating the lease request.
List chats for the owner with unread counters, counterparty info, and car cover photo URL.
Richer chat header with resolved participant names.
Sends a text message. Server broadcasts `new_message` via WS to the other participant. Note: immediate response has `sender_name: Unknown` (cosmetic); WS event carries the correct name.
Reverse-chronological. Returns `sender_name` correctly via DB join.
Resets unread counter for the caller. Broadcasts a read event via WS.
Upload an attachment (image, pdf, video) to the chat. Kind is auto-detected from MIME.
Optional query `kind=image|document|video`.
Status: requested → accepted. Only the owner can accept. Server broadcasts `lease_request_updated` via WS to the driver.
Soft-archives the chat for the caller only.
Returns pending lease requests where the caller is the owner. Used to power the 'Today' home tab. Filtered by `last_seen_actions_at`.
Update last_seen marker so existing actions no longer show as 'unread'.
Legacy endpoint — returns in-chat Requests. Prefer /today/actions for lease requests.

item.name: 09 · Payments & Alternate Branches (Stripe)
item.description: Stripe payment flow plus decline/cancel. Payment endpoints require real Stripe keys; decline/cancel work without Stripe.

request.description: Returns Stripe client_secret + publishable_key + ephemeral_key. REQUIRES `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` env on the backend. Without a key, returns 500 STRIPE_ERROR — that's expected in a local-only demo.
Re-queries Stripe and reconciles payment status locally. Either participant can call.
Status: requested → declined. Only owner can decline.
Creator can cancel anytime before paid. Transitions requested/accepted/payment_pending → cancelled.

item.name: 10 · Optional — Password Reset, Deletes
item.description: Password reset (requires copying the token from the dev console) and delete endpoints for tear-down.

request.description: Dev mode: reset token is printed to the server console. Copy from the '📧 PASSWORD RESET EMAIL' banner into `passwordResetToken`.
Consumes a single-use reset token. Revokes all existing refresh tokens for that user.
Counterparty profile — used in chat screens to show the other person's name/photo.
Deletes the uploaded document and its file on disk.
Deletes the car + cascades photos/documents. Irreversible.

item.name: 11 · WebSocket — How to Connect
item.description:
Postman supports WebSocket requests as a separate request type (New → WebSocket Request).

**URL:** `{{wsUrl}}?token={{accessTokenOwner}}`  (to receive owner events)
or      `{{wsUrl}}?token={{accessTokenDriver}}`  (to receive driver events)

### Events the server broadcasts
- `lease_request_created` — fired when a driver posts a lease request; delivered to the **owner**
- `lease_request_updated` — fired on accept/decline/cancel/paid; delivered to both parties
- `new_message` — fired when a chat message is sent; delivered to the other participant
- `request_created` / `request_updated` — for in-chat Request objects (manual_payment, etc.)

### Live demo recipe
1. In one Postman WebSocket tab, connect as owner using `{{accessTokenOwner}}`.
2. In the collection, run **07 · Create Lease Request** as the driver.
3. The WebSocket tab shows a `lease_request_created` event within ~1 second.
4. Run **Owner Sends Message** — the owner's WS tab echoes nothing (they are the sender). Connect a second WS tab as driver and re-send — it will receive `new_message`.

Keepalive: server sends a WS ping every ~54 s; Postman's built-in handling is fine.
Auth failure: if the token is invalid or expired, upgrade fails with 401.
